name: saab/check_ignition_key_status
purpose: Read the Ignition Key Status block from the engine ECM. Returns a 7-byte DPID payload that exposes how many keys are paired and the key-recognition state.
preconditions: GMLAN session active (Tech2Win sends `FE 3E` TesterPresent on 0x0101 every ~2.5s).
No SecurityAccess (0x27) required for this workflow; DeviceControl 0xAE and ReadDataByPacketIdentifier 0xAA are pre-authorised.

steps.purpose: Handshake / sanity check that the engine ECM is responsive.
DeviceControl DCID=0x01 sub=0x01 — initialise key-status workflow.
Subscribe to DPID 0x01 (engine vitals, baseline context).
DeviceControl DCID=0x03 sub=0x02 — request key-status snapshot.
notes: ECM may return `7F AE 78` (responsePending) before the positive `EE 03`.
Allow ~600 ms for the positive reply.

steps.purpose: Read DPID 0x0B — the key-status result block.
decoding_notes: Byte[1..2] = status / flag pair — observed 0x43 0x80. Bit meaning TBD; diff against `no key` and `valid key` captures to lock down.
Byte[3..4] = paired-key ID / transponder code — observed 0xF7 0x01.

steps.purpose: Read DPID 0x08 — additional context block. Decoding TBD.
Read DPID 0x06 — engine-state context. Decoding TBD.

open_questions: Exact bit layout of DPID 0x0B byte[1..2] (status / flags).
Whether bytes 5..7 of DPID 0x0B carry key-count + slot state under different conditions.
Whether DPID 0x08 / 0x06 are part of this workflow proper or background telemetry Tech2Win polls anyway.
Whether `AE 01 01` is universal init or specific to this workflow.
next_actions: Capture a second run with a different key (or no key) inserted; diff DPID 0x0B byte by byte.
Capture the actual Add Key flow (separate workflow) and look for `27 0B` + a WriteDataByIdentifier / RoutineControl sequence afterward.
